records.ety: [OE. harvest, hervest, AS. hærfest autumn; akin to LG. harfst, D. herfst, OHG. herbist, G. herbst, and prob. to L. carpere to pluck, Gr. karpo`s fruit. Cf. Carpet.]

defs: The gathering of a crop of any kind; the ingathering of the crops; also, the season of gathering grain and fruits, late summer or early autumn.
quotes: Seedtime and harvest . . . shall not cease.
At harvest, when corn is ripe.

defs: That which is reaped or ready to be reaped or gathered; a crop, as of grain (wheat, maize, etc.), or fruit.
quotes: Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe.
To glean the broken ears after the man That the main harvest reaps.

defs: The product or result of any exertion or labor; gain; reward.
quotes: The pope's principal harvest was in the jubilee.
The harvest of a quiet eye.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Harvested; p. pr. & vb. n. Harvesting.]

defs: To reap or gather, as any crop.
